message:
feat(ondisk): refactor ext4 htree lookup to support multi-level indirect indexes

Rewrite the DX htree directory lookup to handle arbitrary indirect
levels instead of hardcoding a single-level inner/leaf structure:

- Replace the flat inner_entries + leaf iteration with a Frame-based
  stack that walks indirect levels from root down to leaf
- Add empty-entries guard at root to fall back to linear lookup
- Loop over indirect_levels to push child frames, resolving extents
  and parsing DX entries at each level before narrowing to the hash
  bucket
- Replace the fixed inner_idx/leaf iteration with a generic level-walk
  that advances the deepest frame first, then pops up when exhausted
- Handle edge cases: empty child entries bail early, missing extent
  mappings return None

This correctly supports ext4 directories with 0, 1, or multiple
indirect DX levels, matching the kernel's dx_probe behavior.

Also flushes beads issue tracker.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
